subject: Geography
title: Gerrymandering Maps
description: Learn Gerrymandering Maps in one minute.

hook.text: In 1812, a Massachusetts district was drawn so oddly that a newspaper said it looked like a salamander.

buildup.text: Governor Elbridge Gerry approved the map—combining his name with 'salamander' gave us 'gerrymander.'

discovery.text: Drawing district lines to favor one party is still legal in most US states.

twist.text: Computers now optimize gerrymandering, creating districts with surgical precision.

climax.text: A map drawn by politicians can predetermine elections before a single vote is cast.

punchline.text: The most powerful map isn't of land. It's of voters.

quiz.question: Where does the word 'gerrymander' come from?
